message:
test(history): update test infrastructure for telemetry→history rename

SUMMARY
=======
Updates all test files and infrastructure to use the new "history" naming
convention, ensuring consistency with the core module rename.

CHANGES BY FILE
===============

tests/common/db.rs:
- Import HistoryDb instead of TelemetryDb
- Import HistoryConfig instead of TelemetryConfig
- Rename create_test_telemetry_db() → create_test_history_db()
- Update comments to reference "history" instead of "telemetry"
- Change default_telemetry_config() → default_history_config()
- Environment variable DCG_TELEMETRY_DB → DCG_HISTORY_DB

tests/common/fixtures.rs:
- Update test fixture creation to use HistoryConfig
- Rename telemetry_config field references to history_config

tests/common/logging.rs:
- Update comments referencing telemetry to history

tests/common/mod.rs:
- Re-export history test helpers with new names
- Update module documentation

tests/telemetry_full_flow.rs:
- Update imports to use history module types
- Rename test helper function calls

tests/telemetry_integration.rs:
- Comprehensive updates to all integration tests
- HistoryDb, HistoryError, HistoryConfig imports
- Test assertions updated for new type names
- Environment variable references updated

tests/e2e/run_telemetry_e2e.sh:
- Update DCG_TELEMETRY_DB → DCG_HISTORY_DB
- Update database filename telemetry.db → history.db
- Update log messages and comments
- Ensure script tests new history CLI subcommands

NOTE: The test file names (telemetry_*.rs) are preserved for now to
maintain git history. A future commit may rename these files to
history_*.rs for full consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","title":"[CATEGORY] remote.* - Remote Access Pack Suite","description":"# Remote Access Pack Suite

## Overview
Remote access tools (SSH, scp, rsync) can execute commands and transfer files to remote systems. Accidental destructive commands on production servers are common footguns.

## Packs to Implement

### remote.ssh
- **Why Critical**: SSH provides direct access to remote systems. Commands executed remotely have same risks as local.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `ssh host 'destructive-command'` - Remote command execution
  - Piping destructive scripts through SSH
  - `ssh-keygen -R` - Removes known hosts (security)
  - Key deletion operations
- **Safe Patterns**: `ssh -V`, `ssh-keygen -l` (fingerprint), connection testing

### remote.scp
- **Why Critical**: scp can overwrite remote files. Wrong direction can overwrite source or dest.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - Overwriting critical remote paths
  - Recursive copy to wrong destination
- **Safe Patterns**: Read-only operations, specific file copies

### remote.rsync
- **Why Critical**: rsync --delete removes files at destination not in source. Common footgun.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `rsync --delete` - Deletes extra files at dest
  - `rsync --delete-before` / `--delete-after`
  - Wrong source/dest direction
- **Safe Patterns**: `--dry-run`, `--list-only`, `-n`

## Special Note
These commands are meta-dangerous because they can execute ANY command remotely. Pattern matching should detect when destructive commands are being tunneled through SSH.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SSH remote command execution analyzed
- [ ] rsync --delete variants detected
- [ ] Dry-run flags recognized as safe
- [ ] Tests cover remote execution patterns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:29:31.744652142-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:09:07.443721232-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:09:07.443721232-05:00","close_reason":"All remote packs implemented: rsync (78a), ssh (1gv), scp (re7) all closed; remote.* category complete","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-78a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:10.544925551-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:01.156321085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-re7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:01.190182549-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.208601095-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.254100032-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.302424925-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.349723839-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.395123369-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-g5o","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:49.439109319-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-gie","title":"Fix dcg: unknown CLI args should error (no stdin hang)","description":"## Problem

`src/main.rs` ignored clap parse errors. Running `dcg --unknown` (or any invalid CLI invocation) fell through to hook-mode and blocked on stdin, which looks like a hang and is confusing UX.

## Fix

- If CLI parsing fails, print the clap error and exit (status 2).
- Keep custom `--help/-h` and `--version/-V` output behavior.
- Hook mode (stdin JSON, no args) remains unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg --this-flag-does-not-exist` exits quickly with a clear error.
- `dcg <subcommand>` continues to work normally.
- Hook invocation via stdin JSON still works.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-08T04:20:37.767980269-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T04:21:49.547802511-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T04:21:49.547802511-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed main CLI dispatch: unknown args now print clap error and exit 2 (no stdin hang)."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-goph","title":"Define Go destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"# Go Destructive Patterns for Heredoc Scanning

## Objective

Define Go heredoc matchers that detect **high-signal destructive operations**, primarily when Go code is used as a wrapper to execute destructive shell commands or delete recursively.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on irreversible filesystem/process operations.
- Do not turn this into a general Go security scanner (exclude unsafe/reflection/network heuristics).

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Process execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- `exec.Command("rm", "-rf", ...).Run()` (literal)
- `exec.Command("bash", "-c", "rm -rf ...")` (literal)
- `exec.Command("git", "reset", "--hard", ... )` (literal)

Avoid low-signal matches:

- `exec.Command($CMD, ...)` should not be a default deny.

### 2) Recursive deletion (high signal)

- `os.RemoveAll("/")` / other clearly catastrophic literal targets → Error
- `os.RemoveAll("./build")` → Warning at most

## False positive mitigation

- Require negative fixtures for:
  - benign command execution (`exec.Command("go", "test")`)
  - benign cleanup under `/tmp`
  - code that prints dangerous strings

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines an initial Go pattern set focused on `exec.Command` + destructive payloads and `os.RemoveAll`.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless the matched operation is clearly catastrophic.

Commit a0a89bd.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-goph","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:46:45.603432505-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-goph","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:51:56.680228592-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-gpej","title":"[EPIC] Command Telemetry Database System","notes":"## Executive Summary

Implement a comprehensive SQLite-based telemetry system that captures EVERY command from EVERY coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI). DCG is uniquely positioned as the universal gatekeeper - this feature leverages that position to provide unprecedented observability into agent behavior.

## Strategic Value

### Why This Matters

1. **Unique Position**: No other tool sees all commands from all agents
2. **Currently Wasted**: DCG evaluates every command but discards allowed ones
3. **User Need**: 'What did the agent do?' is unanswerable today
4. **Project Goal**: DCG should be indispensable, not just a safety net

### Use Cases

1. **Debugging**: 'That session broke my build - what commands ran?'
2. **Security Audit**: Complete trail of all agent activity
3. **Analytics**: Understand agent behavior patterns
4. **Pack Improvement**: Data-driven pattern refinement
5. **Trust Building**: Users can verify what agents did

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     DCG Hook (PreToolUse)                       │
│  ┌──────────┐    ┌────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Parse    │───▶│ Evaluate   │───▶│ Return allow/deny      │  │
│  │ Command  │    │ Command    │    │                        │  │
│  └──────────┘    └─────┬──────┘    └────────────────────────┘  │
│                        │                                        │
│                        ▼ (async, non-blocking)                  │
│               ┌────────────────┐                                │
│               │ Telemetry      │                                │
│               │ Writer         │                                │
│               └───────┬────────┘                                │
│                       │                                         │
└───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────┘
                        │
                        ▼
              ┌─────────────────┐
              │ telemetry.db    │ ~/.config/dcg/telemetry.db
              │ (SQLite)        │
              └────────┬────────┘
                       │
         ┌─────────────┼─────────────┐
         │             │             │
         ▼             ▼             ▼
    ┌─────────┐  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────┐
    │ CLI     │  │ Export   │  │ Analytics│
    │ Queries │  │ JSON/CSV │  │ Stats    │
    └─────────┘  └──────────┘  └──────────┘
```

## Phase Breakdown

### Phase 1: Foundation (P2)
- Schema design
- Database initialization
- Configuration options

### Phase 2: Logging (P2)
- Hook integration
- Async writes
- Redaction options

### Phase 3: Query Interface (P2-P3)
- CLI commands
- Export functionality

### Phase 4: Maintenance (P2)
- Automatic pruning
- Size management

### Phase 5: Analytics (P3)
- Statistics CLI
- Pack effectiveness analysis

## Child Issues

### Foundation
- git_safety_guard-k8n7: Schema design
- git_safety_guard-9asp: Database initialization
- git_safety_guard-joyb: Configuration options

### Logging
- git_safety_guard-7piw: Hook integration
- git_safety_guard-eod3: Redaction options

### Query Interface
- git_safety_guard-kldl: CLI query commands
- git_safety_guard-ulmv: Export options

### Maintenance
- git_safety_guard-chvj: Automatic pruning

### Analytics
- git_safety_guard-30oo: Statistics CLI
- git_safety_guard-lo50: Pack effectiveness analysis

## Success Metrics

1. **Adoption**: Users enable telemetry
2. **Utility**: Users query the database
3. **Performance**: <100μs overhead per command
4. **Reliability**: No crashes, no data loss
5. **Privacy**: Redaction works, no secret leakage

## Dependencies Graph

```
Schema Design (k8n7)
    │
    ▼
DB Initialization (9asp)
    │
    ▼
Configuration (joyb)
    │
    ├──────────────────────┐
    ▼                      ▼
Hook Integration (7piw)    CLI Queries (kldl)
    │                      │
    ▼                      ├───▶ Export (ulmv)
Redaction (eod3)           │
    │                      ├───▶ Stats (30oo)
    │                      │         │
    │                      │         ▼
    │                      │     Pack Analysis (lo50)
    │                      │
    ├──────────────────────┘
    ▼
Pruning (chvj)
```

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Performance impact | Async writes, fail-open |
| Disk space growth | Automatic pruning, size limits |
| Privacy concerns | Redaction options, opt-in |
| Schema changes | Migration system from day 1 |

## Timeline Considerations

This is a significant feature (~10 tasks). Recommended approach:
1. Foundation + Hook Integration first (MVP)
2. Query Interface next (usable)
3. Maintenance (sustainable)
4. Analytics (polish)

## Related Work

- Existing `log_file` for blocks (will continue to work)
- Allow-once store (separate, for bypass tracking)
- cass (session search - complementary, not replacement)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-14T01:23:56.873609714-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T01:23:56.873609714-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-gpej","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-5ju1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T01:47:56.914298469-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","title":"[CATEGORY] platform.gitlab - GitLab Platform Pack","description":"# GitLab Platform Pack

## Overview
GitLab provides integrated DevOps platform with CI/CD, container registry, and infrastructure management. Similar risks to GitHub but with additional features.

## Why This Pack
- GitLab CI/CD variables contain secrets
- Project deletion removes all associated data
- Runner tokens provide CI access
- Protected branch removal exposes main

## Key Destructive Patterns

### glab CLI
- `glab repo delete` - Repository deletion
- `glab variable delete` - Removes CI variables
- `glab release delete` - Deletes releases
- `glab api -X DELETE` - Raw API deletions

### API Patterns
- `DELETE /projects/{id}`
- `DELETE /projects/.../variables/`
- `DELETE /projects/.../hooks/`
- `DELETE /projects/.../protected_branches/`

### gitlab-rails (self-hosted)
- `gitlab-rails runner` with destructive code
- `gitlab-rake` maintenance tasks

## Safe Patterns
- `glab repo view`, `glab project list`
- `glab variable list`
- `glab api` with GET method

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 2 Epic (git_safety_guard-hhh)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] glab CLI patterns comprehensive
- [ ] API endpoint patterns detected
- [ ] Self-hosted admin patterns covered
- [ ] Tests cover common destructive operations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:28:24.685736839-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.163371572-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:05:24.163371572-05:00","close_reason":"platform.gitlab pack implemented and registered, all dependencies closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-z2q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:34:30.648419203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.214582738-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.261309686-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.308009552-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.354369499-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.40221398-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-h5j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:47.449110616-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-hcfg","title":"Add heredoc scanning configuration and CLI flags","description":"# Heredoc Scanning Configuration

## Objective

Allow users to configure heredoc scanning behavior via CLI flags and config file.

## CLI Flags

```
dcg check [OPTIONS]

Heredoc Scanning:
    --heredoc-scan          Enable heredoc scanning (default: true)
    --no-heredoc-scan       Disable heredoc scanning
    --heredoc-timeout <MS>  Timeout for heredoc analysis (default: 50)
    --heredoc-languages <L> Languages to scan (default: all)
                            Values: python,bash,javascript,typescript,ruby,perl
```

## Config File (~/.config/dcg/config.toml)

```toml
[heredoc]
enabled = true
timeout_ms = 50
languages = ["python", "bash", "javascript", "typescript", "ruby", "perl"]

# Graceful degradation
fallback_on_parse_error = true  # Allow if parsing fails
fallback_on_timeout = true       # Allow if timeout exceeded

# Logging
log_heredoc_detection = false    # Log when heredocs detected
log_pattern_matches = false      # Log which patterns matched
```

## Pack Integration

Add heredoc scanning as a pack that can be enabled/disabled:

```rust
// In packs/heredoc.rs
pub fn create_pack() -> Pack {
    Pack {
        id: "heredoc".to_string(),
        name: "Heredoc Scanning",
        description: "Detect destructive patterns in inline scripts and heredocs",
        keywords: &["<<", "-c", "-e", "python", "bash", "node"],
        safe_patterns: vec![],
        destructive_patterns: vec![],  // Patterns defined per-language
    }
}
```

## Environment Variables

- `DCG_HEREDOC_ENABLED=true|false`
- `DCG_HEREDOC_TIMEOUT=50`
- `DCG_HEREDOC_LANGUAGES=python,bash,javascript`

## Default Behavior

- Heredoc scanning: ENABLED by default
- Timeout: 50ms
- Languages: All supported
- Fallback: Allow on error (fail-open for usability)

## Test Cases

- Verify --no-heredoc-scan skips heredoc detection
- Verify timeout is respected
- Verify language filtering works
- Verify config file is loaded correctly
- Verify environment variables override config

## Acceptance Criteria

- CLI flags and config settings map to a single internal config model (no drift).
- Hook mode behavior is safe:
  - timeout/parse failures fail-open (allow) with optional diagnostics.
- `dcg explain` surfaces heredoc config decisions (enabled/disabled/timeout) for debugging.
- Unit tests cover flag parsing + config precedence (CLI > env > config file > defaults).
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive / disruption controls
Heredoc scanning is the highest-FP-risk feature. Config must explicitly support minimizing disruption:

- per-language enable/disable
- strict timeouts + size limits + fail-open in hook mode
- easy disable switch for users/projects that hit noisy edge cases
- allowlist integration (rule-based + content-hash) so teams can suppress known-safe patterns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T10:49:57.809297596-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T10:49:57.809297596-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented [heredoc] config (enabled/limits/language filter) + env overrides + dcg test flags; plumbed into evaluator/hook; added unit tests; ran fmt/check/clippy/test + e2e.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-hcfg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.542568801-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-hcfg","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:51:20.634350127-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-hcj","title":"Fix regex backtracking panic in normalize_command (discovered by fuzzing)","description":"## Summary

Fuzzer discovered a panic when `normalize_command` processes inputs with many consecutive `/` characters.

## Reproduction

```
//////////////////_(rm
```

or bytes with mixed `/` and null characters:
```
[47, 47, 47, 0, 0, 47, 0, 0, 47, 0, 0, 47, 47, 0, 0, 47, 0, 91, ...]
```

## Root Cause

In `src/packs/mod.rs:490`, `normalize_command` uses:

```rust
PATH_NORMALIZER.replace(cmd, "$1")
```

which internally calls `.unwrap()` on the regex result. When the regex engine hits its backtrack limit, this panics:

```
thread 'panicked at fancy-regex-0.14.0/src/lib.rs:1073:45:
called Result::unwrap() on an Err value: RuntimeError(BacktrackLimitExceeded)
```

## Fix

Handle the error gracefully by returning the original command unchanged:

```rust
pub fn normalize_command(cmd: &str) -> Cow<'_, str> {
    if !cmd.starts_with('/') {
        return Cow::Borrowed(cmd);
    }
    // Handle regex errors gracefully (fail-open)
    PATH_NORMALIZER.replace(cmd, "$1").unwrap_or(Cow::Borrowed(cmd))
}
```

## Artifacts

- `fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_evaluate/minimized-from-6116856d9c40a74797b32a4ad3efe166a30f82a7`
- `fuzz/artifacts/fuzz_normalize/minimized-from-c5521321fb46097edf17593ccf4ac7d3c9fed259`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-08T00:54:15.525383593-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T00:58:43.724650343-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T00:58:43.724650343-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: use try_replacen with fail-open behavior. Regression test added."}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","title":"Define Ruby destructive patterns for heredoc scanning","description":"## Objective

Define AST-aware matchers for destructive operations in Ruby heredoc bodies, focusing on high-signal runtime behavior.

## Scope boundary

- Focus on destructive filesystem/process operations.
- Avoid broad “metaprogramming danger” patterns (e.g., `eval`) as default denies; they are common and not necessarily destructive.

## Pattern families (v1)

### 1) Filesystem deletion (high signal)

- `FileUtils.rm_rf(path)` / `FileUtils.remove_dir(path, true)`
- `File.delete(path)` / `File.unlink(path)`

Severity guidance:

- `Error` only for catastrophic literal targets.
- Otherwise default `Warning`.

### 2) Process execution with destructive payload (high signal)

- backticks with literal destructive payload: `` `rm -rf ...` ``
- `system("rm -rf ...")`
- `exec("git reset --hard ...")`

Important:

- Do not flag `system($VAR)` as deny; too broad. At most warn if combined with other signals.

## Testing requirements

- Positive fixtures for:
  - `FileUtils.rm_rf("/")` (catastrophic)
  - `system("rm -rf /")`
- Negative fixtures for:
  - `FileUtils.rm_rf("./tmp")` (warning at most)
  - `puts "rm -rf"`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Defines a Ruby pattern set aligned with severity taxonomy and focused on irreversible operations.
- Includes at least 5 positive and 5 negative fixtures.
- No default hard denies unless clearly catastrophic.

All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:35.430025823-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T18:37:35.449812115-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-mvh","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:51:30.957434297-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-nax","title":"[CATEGORY] search.* - Search Engine Pack Suite","description":"# Search Engine Pack Suite

## Overview
This category covers protection for search and analytics engines. Index deletion, mapping changes, or cluster modifications can cause significant data loss and extended downtime for search functionality.

## Packs to Implement

### search.elasticsearch
- **Why Critical**: Elasticsearch powers search for countless applications. Index deletion is instantaneous and permanent. Mapping changes can corrupt data.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `curl -X DELETE .../index` - Index deletion via REST
  - `DELETE /_all` - Deletes ALL indices
  - `DELETE /*` - Wildcard deletion
  - `POST /_reindex` with destructive dest - Overwrites destination
  - `PUT /_cluster/settings` (dangerous settings)
  - `POST /_close` - Closes indices (makes unavailable)
  - Kibana/DevTools equivalents
- **Safe Patterns**: `GET`, `_search`, `_cat`, `_cluster/health`

### search.opensearch
- **Why Critical**: AWS-managed fork of Elasticsearch with same risks. Many orgs migrated to OpenSearch.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**: Same as Elasticsearch plus:
  - `aws opensearch delete-domain` - Deletes entire domain
  - `aws opensearch delete-inbound-connection`
  - OpenSearch Dashboards destructive operations
- **Safe Patterns**: `describe-`, `list-`, `get-`

### search.algolia
- **Why Critical**: SaaS search with API-based management. Index deletion affects all applications using that index.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `algolia indices delete` - Index deletion
  - `algolia indices clear` - Clears all records
  - `algolia rules delete` - Deletes search rules
  - `algolia synonyms delete` - Deletes synonyms
  - API calls with `deleteIndex`, `clearObjects`
- **Safe Patterns**: `browse`, `search`, `list`, `get`

### search.meilisearch
- **Why Critical**: Growing alternative to Algolia/Elasticsearch. Same index deletion risks.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `curl -X DELETE .../indexes/` - Index deletion
  - `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` - Document deletion
  - `POST /dumps` (with overwrite)
- **Safe Patterns**: `GET`, `/search`, `/stats`

## Rationale
Search indices often take hours or days to rebuild from source data. During that time, search functionality is degraded or unavailable. Some search systems (especially SaaS) don't have point-in-time recovery, making deletion truly permanent.

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 1 Epic (git_safety_guard-6ae)
- Blocked by: None (can start immediately)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All four packs implemented with comprehensive patterns
- [ ] REST API patterns detected in curl/httpie commands
- [ ] CLI tool patterns for each search engine
- [ ] Tests cover wildcard deletion patterns
- [ ] Documentation warns about index rebuild times","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:27:17.807248038-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T23:03:00.261905192-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T23:03:00.261905192-05:00","close_reason":"All -{"id":"git_safety_guard-nmi6","title":"[EPIC] Allow-Once UX Improvements","notes":"## Summary

Improve the allow-once bypass code user experience. The current system works but users report they've NEVER SEEN the codes, indicating a critical visibility problem.

## Problem Statement

When dcg blocks a command, it outputs a short code that enables temporary bypass. However:
1. The code uses hex characters (harder to type than numbers)
2. The code is not visually prominent in terminal output
3. Users don't notice it, making the safety-valve mechanism useless

## Child Issues

- git_safety_guard-z72c: Switch to numeric-only codes (5 digits)
- git_safety_guard-3dg8: Add bold/color formatting for visibility

## Impact

If users can't easily see/use bypass codes:
- They get frustrated with 'false positives'
- They disable dcg entirely
- The safety-valve design fails

Making codes visible and easy to use is critical for adoption.

## No Dependencies

These are standalone UX improvements that can be done in any order or in parallel.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-14T01:24:45.984797292-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T01:24:45.984797292-05:00"}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-nwu","title":"Bug: build dir removals incorrectly allowed by quick-reject","description":"## Problem

Commands like `rm -rf build`, `rm -rf target`, `rm -rf dist`, and `rm -rf node_modules` are being ALLOWED when they should be BLOCKED by core.filesystem.

This is a regression where the quick-reject logic (`pack_aware_quick_reject`) incorrectly allows these commands.

## Expected Behavior

With only `core.filesystem` enabled (and `safe.cleanup` disabled):
- These common build directory removals should be BLOCKED
- `rm -rf src` should be BLOCKED (this one works correctly)
- `rm -rf foo` should be BLOCKED (this one works correctly)

## Actual Behavior

- Build directory removals (build, target, dist, node_modules) are ALLOWED
- `rm -rf src` is correctly BLOCKED
- `rm -rf foo` is correctly BLOCKED

## Root Cause (suspected)

The `pack_aware_quick_reject` function in `src/packs/mod.rs` appears to be incorrectly classifying these commands as not containing relevant keywords in executable spans.

The unit test `blocks_rm_rf_outside_safe_dirs` passes because it directly tests the pattern matching without going through the quick-reject logic.

## Evidence

```
dcg explain "rm -rf build"
Decision: ALLOW
Pipeline Trace: quick-rejected (no keywords)

dcg explain "rm -rf src"
Decision: DENY
Pack: core.filesystem
Pattern: rm-rf-general
```

Both commands contain "rm" but only "src" is correctly evaluated.

## Files to investigate

- src/packs/mod.rs: pack_aware_quick_reject function
- src/context.rs: classify_command / executable_spans
- src/evaluator.rs: evaluate_command_with_pack_order_deadline_at_path

## Impact

E2E tests for rm -rf on build directories are failing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:09:00.561192844-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:57.068568873-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T14:18:57.068568873-05:00","close_reason":"Bug was a stale binary issue. Rebuild fixed it. Added regression tests (pack_aware_quick_reject_rm_commands_not_rejected, full_flow_core_category_rm_commands_blocked). All 204 E2E tests pass."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-nys","title":"[PACK] cdn.cloudflare_workers - Cloudflare Workers Pack","description":"# Cloudflare Workers Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Cloudflare Workers and KV operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### Workers
- `wrangler delete` - Deletes Worker
- `wrangler deployments rollback` (can break things)

### KV
- `wrangler kv:key delete`
- `wrangler kv:namespace delete`
- `wrangler kv:bulk delete`

### R2
- `wrangler r2 object delete`
- `wrangler r2 bucket delete`

### D1 (Database)
- `wrangler d1 delete`

## Safe Patterns
- `wrangler whoami`
- `wrangler kv:key get`, `wrangler kv:key list`
- `wrangler r2 object get`
- `wrangler tail`
- `wrangler dev`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cdn.* (git_safety_guard-wso)
- Keywords: wrangler
- Note: Workers are edge compute, KV is edge storage

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worker deletion blocked
- [ ] KV namespace/key deletion blocked
- [ ] R2 deletion blocked
- [ ] D1 deletion blocked
- [ ] Tests cover common Wrangler operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:35:09.696316475-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:59:59.71856052-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:59:59.71856052-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented cdn.cloudflare_workers pack with 12 safe patterns and 8 destructive patterns covering Workers, KV, R2, and D1 operations via Wrangler CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.4077607-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.453653508-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.502342657-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.54949762-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.596835677-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.642547104-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.456207928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-nys","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.504506124-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
